headline_summary: U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran have escalated into a multi-day regional conflict with direct impacts on energy infrastructure and shipping.
Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and warned it will fire on any ship attempting to pass; multiple vessels have been hit.
Major producers and facilities have taken precautionary shutdowns (Qatar LNG, Saudi Ras Tanura refinery, Israeli gas fields, Iraqi Kurdistan output), tightening supply.
Container lines (Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd, CMA CGM, MSC) are rerouting/pausing Suez/Bab el-Mandeb transits and halting Hormuz crossings; war-risk surcharges and insurer cover cancellations add costs.
Oil, European gas and shipping rates have surged; broader global markets have moved risk-off amid inflation and growth concerns.
risk_drivers: Threatened/prospective closure of the Strait of Hormuz (critical chokepoint for oil/LNG)
Direct attacks on tankers and energy infrastructure across Gulf states
Precautionary shutdowns at major oil/gas facilities (Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli gas fields, Iraqi production)
Spillover into Lebanon/Hezbollah front raising multi-theatre escalation risk
War-risk insurance withdrawal and extreme freight-rate repricing disrupting physical markets

Energy: Higher oil risk premium; potential for further upside if Hormuz closure persists or more Gulf infrastructure is hit.
European gas prices sensitive to LNG supply outages (Qatar) and shipping constraints; volatility elevated.
Refining/product spreads can widen if regional refineries/ports face disruptions (e.g., Ras Tanura, Fujairah).
FX: Risk-off flows can pressure EM FX, especially energy importers in Asia; safe-haven demand may support USD/JPY/CHF.
Gulf currency pegs may hold but local liquidity/credit conditions could tighten if disruption extends.
Rates: Higher energy prices raise near-term inflation risk, complicating DM central bank paths and lifting term premium.
Wider credit spreads possible on geopolitical shock + growth risk; funding costs rise for shipping/energy-linked credits.
Equities: Broad risk-off with underperformance in airlines/transport/chemicals; relative support for energy producers and some defence names.
Higher input and freight costs could hit margins across global industrial and consumer supply chains.

sources.title: US and Israel launch "pre-emptive" attack against Iran
Iran vows to attack any ship trying to pass through Strait of Hormuz
Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli oil, gas fields down due to Mideast strikes
Shipping companies divert vessels around Cape of Good Hope after strikes on Iran
Global oil and gas shipping costs surge as Iran vows to close Strait of Hormuz
Iran war enters fourth day in 'smoke and blood' as markets slide
Oil rises as expanding US-Israeli conflict with Iran elevates supply risks
Exclusive: India reduces gas supply to industries after Qatar outage, sources say
Global energy costs soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, oil and gas production
Marine insurers cancel war risk cover, tanker costs to rise as Iran conflict intensifies

events.title: U.S. and Israel launch 'pre-emptive' strikes on Iran
events.implication: Sharp escalation into direct state-on-state conflict; sets conditions for retaliation impacting energy flows and regional risk premia.

events.title: Iran conflict disrupts global shipping as tankers are stranded and damaged
events.implication: Operational disruption (delays/stranding) reduces effective tanker capacity and tightens prompt supply logistics, reinforcing energy and freight volatility.

events.title: Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz closed; threatens to fire on ships attempting passage
events.implication: Raises risk of immediate disruption to a major global oil/LNG chokepoint, embedding an outsized geopolitical supply premium in crude and shipping.

events.title: Three tankers damaged in the Gulf amid U.S.-Iran conflict escalation
events.implication: Direct kinetic risk to maritime trade; likely pushes war-risk premia, raises freight, and tightens prompt physical crude availability.

events.title: Marine insurers cancel war-risk cover as Iran conflict intensifies
events.implication: Coverage withdrawal can strand vessels, reduce effective tanker supply, and amplify price moves in oil/LNG and freight derivatives.

events.title: Major container lines pause/divert Red Sea and Suez transits after strikes on Iran
events.implication: Longer routes via Cape of Good Hope extend delivery times and raise global freight costs; inflationary impulse broadens beyond energy.

events.title: Middle East oil and gas shipping costs surge to record levels
events.implication: Freight and insurance repricing transmits the shock into refined products and LNG delivered costs, increasing near-term inflation pressure.

events.title: Saudi Aramco shuts Ras Tanura refinery after drone strike (source)
events.implication: Refinery outage risks product shortages and wider crack spreads; adds to the crude price risk premium if sustained.

events.title: Qatar LNG output disrupted amid regional strikes
events.implication: Cuts flexible LNG supply into Europe/Asia, increasing gas price volatility and potential downstream industrial curtailments.

events.title: Israel temporarily shuts key gas fields as conflict escalates
events.implication: Reduces regional gas supply and can affect LNG/pipe flows to neighbours; supports higher regional power and gas prices.

events.title: Iraqi Kurdistan oil output disrupted amid wider regional strikes
events.implication: Additional supply-side tightening reinforces the global crude risk premium and heightens uncertainty around near-term balances.

events.title: India reduces gas supply to some industries after Qatar outage (sources)
events.implication: Shows downstream demand destruction/curtailment risk in LNG-importing economies; negative for growth-sensitive assets.

events.title: Oil rises as expanding U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran elevates supply risks
events.implication: Market pricing reflects higher probability of supply disruption; reinforces inflation concerns and risk-off positioning.

events.title: Global energy costs surge as Iran crisis disrupts shipping and production
events.implication: Higher delivered energy costs can slow growth and lift headline inflation, pressuring equities and widening credit spreads.

events.title: Netanyahu says war with Iran will not take years as conflict enters fourth day
events.implication: Signals intent and duration expectations; markets watch for off-ramps vs. multi-week campaign risk that could deepen supply and shipping impacts.

links.to: Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz closed; threatens to fire on ships attempting passage

links.to: Three tankers damaged in the Gulf amid U.S.-Iran conflict escalation
links.why: A declared closure/threat environment increases likelihood of attacks/incidents against tankers operating near the chokepoint.

links.from: Three tankers damaged in the Gulf amid U.S.-Iran conflict escalation
links.to: Marine insurers cancel war-risk cover as Iran conflict intensifies
links.why: Physical damage events are a direct trigger for insurers raising rates or withdrawing cover.

links.from: Marine insurers cancel war-risk cover as Iran conflict intensifies
links.to: Middle East oil and gas shipping costs surge to record levels
links.why: Reduced cover/greater war-risk premia increase total voyage costs and constrain available tonnage.

links.from: Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz closed; threatens to fire on ships attempting passage
links.why: Hormuz is a critical chokepoint for oil and LNG; closure threats typically lift crude risk premium.

links.from: Middle East oil and gas shipping costs surge to record levels
links.why: Higher delivered energy/freight costs can lift inflation expectations and widen credit spreads on shock risk.

links.from: Global energy costs surge as Iran crisis disrupts shipping and production
links.why: Energy price spikes and supply uncertainty historically trigger broad risk-off positioning.
Risk-off conditions often weaken EM FX, especially for energy importers facing worse terms of trade.
